text.title: Cross-Module Communication Patterns
text.summary: Two communication styles available in a modular monolith: synchronous direct calls (simple, strongly consistent) and asynchronous events (decoupled, eventually consistent). Start with direct calls; introduce events for cross-cutting or async concerns.

section.title: Synchronous Direct Calls
section.p: In-process method calls through the module's public API. The simplest and most common pattern.

rule.statement: Use synchronous calls when the caller needs the result immediately and strong consistency (same database transaction) is required.
rule.rationale: Direct calls are type-safe, debuggable, and have no serialization overhead. They are the default for most cross-module interactions.

example.code:
# payments/services.py from orders.api import get_order # Public API import — correct
def process_payment(order_id: int): order = get_order(order_id) # In-process call, no network # Process payment using order data...

list.item: Pros: simple, type-safe, debuggable, same transaction possible
Cons: tighter coupling, harder to extract to microservices later

section.title: Asynchronous Events
section.p: Modules publish domain events to an in-memory event bus; other modules subscribe to events they care about.

rule.statement: Use events when the caller does not need an immediate result, when eventual consistency is acceptable, or when you are preparing for future microservices extraction.
rule.rationale: Events decouple publisher from subscriber. The Orders module does not need to know that Notifications or Analytics exist. When extracting to microservices, in-memory events map cleanly to Kafka/RabbitMQ messages.

list.item: Pros: loose coupling, easy microservices extraction, fan-out to multiple subscribers
Cons: eventual consistency, harder to debug, idempotency handling required

rule.statement: Start with direct calls. Introduce events when: (1) multiple modules must react to the same fact, (2) the operation is fire-and-forget (audit, notifications), (3) you are actively preparing to extract a module.
rule.rationale: Premature event-driven design adds eventual consistency complexity before the team has the operational maturity to handle it.

list.item: Multiple consumers of the same fact → event
